dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Verified live behavior with `go run ./cmd/furrow validate ownership ... --json`: returned `in_scope` with `matched_deliverable`/`matched_glob`, `out_of_scope` with `ownership_outside_scope` envelope (`severity: warn`, `confirmation_path: warn-with-confirm`), `not_applicable` for canonical artifacts, and focused-row fallback worked when `--row` was omitted.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: `internal/cli/validate_ownership_test.go` covers in-scope, out-of-scope, no-focused-row, missing-row, multi-deliverable first-match behavior, nested `**` glob matching, canonical-artifact carve-out, and focused-row fallback; dispatcher coverage exists in `TestRunValidateDispatcher`.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: `internal/cli/validate.go` registers `ownership` alongside `definition`, `internal/cli/app.go` delegates top-level `validate` to `runValidate`, and `internal/cli/validate_ownership.go` is step-agnostic (no `state.json.step` read, only focused-row/default-row resolution plus `definition.yaml` parsing).

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: The final row definition gives D2 ownership of `internal/cli/validate.go` and `internal/cli/app.go`, and the mixed follow-up commits explicitly document non-D2 file touches as D1/D4/D5/D6 review-fix work rather than unexplained spillover.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: `go test ./...` passed, targeted ownership tests passed, `gofmt -l internal/cli/{validate.go,validate_ownership.go,validate_ownership_test.go,app.go}` returned no output, and the implementation follows the repo’s Go CLI conventions.
